title: Dorchester woman sentenced for fatal stabbing stemming from online feud
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alyssa Partsch, 33, sentenced to 15-20 years for manslaughter in fatal stabbing of mother Jazreanna Sheppard in Boston.
The stabbing occurred after an online feud; Partsch and Sheppard had not met in person before the incident.
Sheppard was stabbed at Park Street MBTA station in July 2023; Partsch was arrested months later in November 2023.

title: NBA superstar’s message to Connecticut Sun fans: ‘They’ll be alright, they’ve got UConn’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Connecticut Sun relocate to Houston, rebranded as the Houston Comets, ending New England's WNBA presence.
Kevin Durant expressed support for the Comets' return, citing UConn's success to ease Connecticut fans' disappointment.
Despite a higher bid from former Celtics minority owner, league chose Houston's $300 million offer for relocation.

title: Alex Cora declines to elaborate after scratching Carlos Narvaez hours before Red Sox game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora scratched Carlos Narvaez from the lineup hours before their game against the Astros.
Cora refused to elaborate if the lineup change was disciplinary, stating it was a necessary decision.
Backup catcher Connor Wong replaced Narvaez and is hitting .500 early in the season despite the team's rough start.

title: Mike Vrabel says Patriots will pick up Christian Gonzalez’s fifth-year option
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel confirms intention to pick up Christian Gonzalez's $18.1 million fifth-year option for 2027.
Christian Gonzalez missed game time in early seasons due to injuries but played 16 games in 2024.
Gonzalez, a 2023 first-round pick, earned a Pro Bowl nod and was second-team All-Pro in 2024.

title: Elias Lindholm, like the Bruins, is getting hot at the ideal time
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Elias Lindholm scored seven points in his last six games, aiding the Bruins' recent winning streak.
Lindholm improved his faceoff win rate to 62.2% while transitioning to a third-line role after an Olympic slump.
David Pastrnak recorded a season-high 66 assists as the Bruins offense gears up for the playoffs.

title: For this Everett runner, the Boston Marathon has always been personal
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Michelle Davidson, 33, from Everett, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with the Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation.
Inspired by the 2013 bombings, she seeks to support teams advancing trauma care and honoring Boston's resilience.
Davidson's personal connection stems from a family impacted by the bombings, reinforcing the race's significance for her.

title: Boston faces budget deficit of nearly $50 million
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston faces a $48.4 million budget deficit due to snow removal, police overtime, and employee health insurance costs.
Mayor Wu's administration is freezing spending and delaying hiring to address the deficit, which is 1% of the $4.8 billion budget.
Councilor Flynn criticized spending increases and called for a commission to address revenue issues and office vacancies.

title: Squirrels, spinach doughnuts, and sirens: New England’s best April Fools’ jokes
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: New England celebrated April Fools' with various pranks, including fictional squirrels and unusual spinach doughnuts.
Local businesses and media outlets participated in crafting humorous and inventive April Fools' jokes.
April Fools' pranks in the region featured creative spins on everyday items and situations.

title: Where do the ‘Real Housewives of Rhode Island’ live?
author: Cameron Sperance
bullets: 'Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2, 2026, featuring cast members Carmody, McGraw, DiMare, Iaconetti, Pontarelli, Swanson, and Tiberi.
Cranston, with its diverse architecture and parcel sizes, serves as a key filming location for the show's Rhode Island settings.
East Greenwich, Newport, and the East Side of Providence are highlighted as aspirational locations in the reality TV series.

title: She’s running Boston for the hospital that saved her daughter’s life
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Leslie Wallus, 44, runs the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Massachusetts General Hospital's pediatric oncology unit.
Wallus's daughter, Eloise, survived a brain tumor thanks to treatment at Massachusetts General Hospital.
Training for the marathon is a personal reflection on her daughter's medical journey and the support from hospital staff.
